scenario_reason: ADCD CV NMSE (0.3856) >= Simple MOND (2-param) (0.3774); ADCD does not beat MOND out of sample, so the in-sample BIC gap reflects an OOS fit-quality difference rather than a complexity penalty alone.
reviewer_message: Out-of-sample, ADCD and Simple MOND (2-param) are statistically indistinguishable: CV NMSE 0.3856 vs 0.3774 (delta +0.0083, z=+0.39, Not significant OOS [ADCD loses]). The reported in-sample BIC gap thus reflects an OOS fit-quality difference of ~0.008 NMSE, not a complexity penalty alone; the SPARC result should be framed as autonomous-discovery *parity* with MOND rather than predictive *superiority*.
